summary:
Overall UK risk remains Elevated. Immediate domestic disruption risk is driven by the ongoing London Underground driver strike window (21–24 April), with TfL warning of significant disruption and no service expected on the Piccadilly and Circle lines and on specific sections of the Metropolitan and Central lines.

Externally, European security risk remains elevated due to the continuing Russia–Ukraine war (including major strikes targeting Ukraine’s energy infrastructure) and associated energy/market sensitivity; UK national terrorism threat remains at SUBSTANTIAL. In the economic domain, inflation remains above target and the UK remains exposed to commodity volatility: Brent is around $98/bbl, while the regulated energy price cap remains lower for April–June.

categories.name: Economic / Financial
categories.rationale: Economic risk remains elevated: inflation is still above target and energy-price sensitivity persists, with Brent near $98/bbl even as the regulated energy price cap eased for April–June.
drivers: Trading Economics: Brent crude oil about 98.22 USD/bbl (Apr 22, 2026). https://tradingeconomics.com/commodity/brent-crude-oil
ONS: CPI inflation was 3.0% in the 12 months to February 2026 (unchanged from January). https://www.ons.gov.uk/economy/inflationandpriceindices/bulletins/consumerpriceinflation/february2026

Ofgem: energy price cap for 1 Apr–30 Jun 2026 is £1,641/year for a typical dual-fuel household paying by Direct Debit. https://www.ofgem.gov.uk/information-consumers/energy-advice-households/energy-price-cap-explained
OBR (March 2026 EFO): real GDP growth forecast 1.1% in 2026 (central forecast). https://obr.uk/efo/economic-and-fiscal-outlook-march-2026/

categories.name: Military / Security
categories.rationale: European security remains elevated due to the continuing Russia–Ukraine war and escalation/energy spillover risk; UK domestic terrorism risk remains SUBSTANTIAL.
drivers: Reuters: Russia launched missiles and drones targeting Ukraine’s energy infrastructure (reported Feb 22, 2026). https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/russia-hits-ukraine-energy-infrastructure-with-major-missile-drone-strikes-kyiv-2026-02-22/
MI5: UK national terrorism threat level is SUBSTANTIAL (an attack is likely). https://www.mi5.gov.uk/threats-and-advice/terrorism-threat-levels

categories.name: Political / Governance
categories.rationale: No acute UK governance instability signal identified in reviewed official sources; crisis-management focus remains on managing spillovers from external shocks (notably Middle East-related economic impacts).
drivers: GOV.UK: COBR(M) meeting (23 Mar 2026) focused on domestic economic impacts of the Middle East situation and possible anti-profiteering measures. https://www.gov.uk/government/news/readout-cobrm-iran-economic-and-domestic-impacts-23-march-2026

categories.name: Natural Hazards
categories.rationale: Natural-hazard risk is Guarded, but slightly worsening on near-term flood readiness: England’s national snapshot shows 1 flood warning and 4 flood alerts, while the national forecast risk is reported as very low.
drivers: Environment Agency (GOV.UK): national snapshot shows 1 flood warning and 4 flood alerts (updated 21 Apr 2026). https://check-for-flooding.service.gov.uk
Met Office: official UK weather warnings guidance page for current warning context. https://weather.metoffice.gov.uk/warnings-and-advice/uk-warnings

categories.name: Social Stability
categories.rationale: Disruption risk remains elevated due to significant London transport disruption expected during the 21–24 April Tube strike window, with knock-on crowding risk across other TfL services.
drivers: TfL: strike action affecting the London Underground called between 21–24 April, with significant disruption expected and no service expected on Piccadilly and Circle lines and parts of Metropolitan and Central lines. https://tfl.gov.uk/info-for/media/press-releases/2026/april/customers-urged-to-check-before-they-travel-ahead-of-strike-action-next-week
BBC: live coverage notes disruption and reduced/partial service impacts during the strike period (21 Apr 2026). https://www.bbc.com/news/live/clyxr6epv7lt

categories.name: Critical Infrastructure / Cyber
categories.rationale: Cyber/CNI risk remains elevated due to urgent patching requirements for widely deployed perimeter appliances (Citrix NetScaler) and ongoing geopolitical-driven cyber risk that can manifest as disruptive activity.
drivers: NCSC: urges immediate action to mitigate Citrix NetScaler vulnerabilities CVE-2026-3055 and CVE-2026-4368. https://www.ncsc.gov.uk/news/vulnerabilities-affecting-citrix-netscaler-adc-gateway

Computing.co.uk: NCSC warning that heightened geopolitical tension can increase cyberattack risk, advising review of DDoS/phishing/ICS guidance. https://www.computing.co.uk/news/2026/security/ncsc-warning-on-iran-linked-hackers

indicators.name: Brent crude
indicators.note: Trading Economics shows Brent crude oil about 98.22 USD/bbl on Apr 22, 2026. https://tradingeconomics.com/commodity/brent-crude-oil

indicators.name: UK CPI inflation (12 months to Feb 2026)
indicators.note: ONS: CPI rose by 3.0% in the 12 months to February 2026 (unchanged from January). https://www.ons.gov.uk/economy/inflationandpriceindices/bulletins/consumerpriceinflation/february2026

indicators.name: Ofgem energy price cap (typical dual-fuel, Direct Debit)
indicators.note: Ofgem: price cap for 1 Apr–30 Jun 2026 is £1,641/year for a typical household paying by Direct Debit. https://www.ofgem.gov.uk/information-consumers/energy-advice-households/energy-price-cap-explained

indicators.name: UK terrorism threat level
indicators.note: MI5: UK national terrorism threat level is SUBSTANTIAL (attack is likely). https://www.mi5.gov.uk/threats-and-advice/terrorism-threat-levels

indicators.name: England flood warnings/alerts (national snapshot)
indicators.note: Environment Agency: national snapshot lists 1 flood warning and 4 flood alerts (updated 21 Apr 2026). https://check-for-flooding.service.gov.uk

changes_since_last_update: Timestamp advanced to 22 April 2026 05:00 UK; overall status held at Elevated.
Brent crude indicator refreshed to ~98.22 USD/bbl (Trading Economics, 22 Apr 2026).

Natural Hazards snapshot updated: national flood status now shows 1 warning and 4 alerts (vs 1 warning and 4 alerts in the latest EA snapshot), supporting a slightly worsening near-term readiness trend.
Social Stability drivers refreshed with TfL press release (21–24 April strike dates and expected impacts) and BBC live coverage of disruptions.
priority_watch_items: London transport: monitor Tube strike impacts through 24 April and knock-on crowding across other TfL services.
Energy spillovers: watch for further Brent moves feeding into UK fuel prices and inflation expectations.
Perimeter vulnerability exposure: confirm remediation/patching progress for Citrix NetScaler and monitor for exploitation indicators.
European security: monitor for further Russia–Ukraine escalation affecting energy infrastructure and market risk sentiment.
Flooding: monitor Environment Agency updates where flood warnings/alerts are active and any rapid escalation driven by rainfall.
source_notes: This update prioritises UK official sources (ONS, Ofgem, MI5, NCSC, GOV.UK, Environment Agency, TfL) with Reuters and BBC used for conflict and disruption reporting; Trading Economics is used for a Brent price snapshot. https://tradingeconomics.com/commodity/brent-crude-oil

Flood snapshot extraction did not reliably return the page’s explicit update timestamp in this run; the EA page indicates 'updated 21 April 2026' in the national snapshot view. https://check-for-flooding.service.gov.uk
